messages.content: You are an SCBE-AETHERMOORE GeoSeal coding agent. When asked for CLI usage, return exact GeoSeal commands, required flags, output files, and safe sequencing. Do not invent flags.
How do I use GeoSeal `execution-shell`? Give the exact command and required flags.

Command: execution-shell
Purpose: Build and optionally persist a governed execution shell from inline content or a source file.
Exact example:
python -m src.geoseal_cli execution-shell --content "def add(a, b): return a + b" --language python --source-name adder.py --output-dir artifacts/runtime_shell --json

Expected outputs: execution_shell.json, workflow_state.json, run_history_index.json

Relevant help:
usage: geoseal execution-shell [-h] (--source-file SOURCE_FILE |
                               --content CONTENT) [--language LANGUAGE]
                               [--source-name SOURCE_NAME]
                               [--include-extended] [--deck-size DECK_SIZE]
                               [--branch-width BRANCH_WIDTH]
                               [--output-dir OUTPUT_DIR] [--json]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --source-file SOURCE_FILE
                        Path to the source file or brief to route
  --content CONTENT     Inline source content or brief
  --language LANGUAGE   Primary language lane for tokenizer routing
  --source-name SOURCE_NAME
                        Source label for --content mode
  --include-extended    Build with GO and ZI transport overlays enabled
  --deck-size DECK_SIZE
                        Maximum system cards in the bounded deck
  --branch-width BRANCH_WIDTH
                        Near-domain projection width for honeycomb checks
  --output-dir OUTPUT_DIR
                        Optional directory to write execution_shell.json
  --json                Emit machine-readable JSON result

metadata.purpose: Build and optionally persist a governed execution shell from inline content or a source file.

messages.content: You are an SCBE-AETHERMOORE GeoSeal coding agent. When asked for CLI usage, return exact GeoSeal commands, required flags, output files, and safe sequencing. Do not invent flags.
How do I use GeoSeal `run-route`? Give the exact command and required flags.

Command: run-route
Purpose: Execute or resume a routed payload from an existing execution_shell.json or fresh source.
Exact example:
python -m src.geoseal_cli run-route --shell-file artifacts/runtime_shell/execution_shell.json --output-dir artifacts/runtime_shell --json

Expected outputs: route_run.json, workflow_state.json, run_history_index.json

Relevant help:
usage: geoseal run-route [-h] (--shell-file SHELL_FILE |
                         --source-file SOURCE_FILE | --content CONTENT)
                         [--language LANGUAGE] [--source-name SOURCE_NAME]
                         [--include-extended] [--deck-size DECK_SIZE]
                         [--branch-width BRANCH_WIDTH] [--tongue TONGUE]
                         [--timeout TIMEOUT] [--output-dir OUTPUT_DIR]
                         [--json]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --shell-file SHELL_FILE
                        Path to an existing execution_shell.json payload
  --source-file SOURCE_FILE
                        Path to the source file or brief to route and execute
  --content CONTENT     Inline source content or brief
  --language LANGUAGE   Primary language lane for tokenizer routing
  --source-name SOURCE_NAME
                        Source label for --content mode
  --include-extended    Build with GO and ZI transport overlays enabled
  --deck-size DECK_SIZE
                        Maximum system cards in the bounded deck
  --branch-width BRANCH_WIDTH
                        Near-domain projection width for honeycomb checks
  --tongue TONGUE       Optional execution tongue override
  --timeout TIMEOUT     Execution timeout in seconds
  --output-dir OUTPUT_DIR
                        Optional directory to write execution shell, workflow
                        state, and route run records
  --json                Emit machine-readable JSON result

metadata.purpose: Execute or resume a routed payload from an existing execution_shell.json or fresh source.

messages.content: You are an SCBE-AETHERMOORE GeoSeal coding agent. When asked for CLI usage, return exact GeoSeal commands, required flags, output files, and safe sequencing. Do not invent flags.
How do I use GeoSeal `code-graph`? Give the exact command and required flags.

Command: code-graph
Purpose: Analyze Python files and emit a structural code graph as JSON, Mermaid, or DOT.
Exact example:
python -m src.geoseal_cli code-graph --path src/geoseal_cli.py --include-calls --format mermaid

Expected outputs: modules, classes, functions, imports, optional call edges

Relevant help:
usage: geoseal code-graph [-h] --path PATH [--include-calls]
                          [--max-files MAX_FILES]
                          [--format {json,mermaid,dot}]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --path PATH           File or directory to analyze
  --include-calls       Include intra-file call edges (resolved to in-package
                        symbols when possible)
  --max-files MAX_FILES
                        Maximum number of .py files to parse when --path is a
                        directory
  --format {json,mermaid,dot}
                        Output format for the code graph

metadata.purpose: Analyze Python files and emit a structural code graph as JSON, Mermaid, or DOT.

messages.content: You are an SCBE-AETHERMOORE GeoSeal coding agent. When asked for CLI usage, return exact GeoSeal commands, required flags, output files, and safe sequencing. Do not invent flags.
How do I use GeoSeal `command-harmony-map`? Give the exact command and required flags.

Command: command-harmony-map
Purpose: Map code-primary commands across language lanes, binary slots, music-style pitch classes, shared runtime outputs, and training focus records.
Exact example:
python -m src.geoseal_cli command-harmony-map --commands default --tongues KO,AV --execute --training-jsonl-output training-data/sft/geoseal_command_harmony_v1.sft.jsonl --output artifacts/command_harmony/default_primary_exec.json --json

Expected outputs: command harmony report, optional SFT JSONL training rows

Relevant help:
usage: geoseal command-harmony-map [-h] [--commands COMMANDS]
                                   [--tongues TONGUES] [--execute]
                                   [--timeout TIMEOUT] [--include-extended]
                                   [--output OUTPUT]
                                   [--training-jsonl-output TRAINING_JSONL_OUTPUT]
                                   [--json]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --commands COMMANDS   Comma-separated command keys, 'default', or 'all'
  --tongues TONGUES     Comma-separated tongues, 'primary' for KO,AV, or 'all'
  --execute             Run runnable language lanes and group commands by
                        shared stdout
  --timeout TIMEOUT     Per-language runtime timeout in seconds
  --include-extended    Build against the panel with GO and ZI overlays
                        enabled
  --output OUTPUT       Optional JSON report path
  --training-jsonl-output TRAINING_JSONL_OUTPUT
                        Optional SFT JSONL output path for coding-agent
                        training records
  --json                Emit machine-readable JSON result

metadata.purpose: Map code-primary commands across language lanes, binary slots, music-style pitch classes, shared runtime outputs, and training focus records.

messages.content: You are an SCBE-AETHERMOORE GeoSeal coding agent. When asked for CLI usage, return exact GeoSeal commands, required flags, output files, and safe sequencing. Do not invent flags.
How do I use GeoSeal `nexus-dispatch`? Give the exact command and required flags.

Command: nexus-dispatch
Purpose: Package a source payload or brief for the Nexus Hub lane and optionally execute the route.
Exact example:
python -m src.geoseal_cli nexus-dispatch --hub-url "https://hub.hoagsinc.com" --content "def add(a, b): return a + b" --language python --execute --json

Expected outputs: nexus_dispatch_packet.json, nexus_scratchpad.md, optional route_run.json

Relevant help:
usage: geoseal nexus-dispatch [-h] (--source-file SOURCE_FILE |
                              --content CONTENT) [--workspace WORKSPACE]
                              [--output-dir OUTPUT_DIR]
                              [--manifest-file MANIFEST_FILE]
                              [--hub-url HUB_URL]
                              [--token-env-var TOKEN_ENV_VAR]
                              [--language LANGUAGE]
                              [--source-name SOURCE_NAME] [--include-extended]
                              [--deck-size DECK_SIZE]
                              [--branch-width BRANCH_WIDTH] [--execute]
                              [--timeout TIMEOUT] [--json]

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --source-file SOURCE_FILE
                        Path to the source file or brief to dispatch
  --content CONTENT     Inline source content or brief
  --workspace WORKSPACE
                        Workspace directory
  --output-dir OUTPUT_DIR
                        Directory to write the Nexus dispatch bundle
  --manifest-file MANIFEST_FILE
                        Optional direct path to nexus_hub_manifest.json
  --hub-url HUB_URL     Base Nexus Hub URL when no manifest exists yet
  --token-env-var TOKEN_ENV_VAR
                        Environment variable name recorded in new manifests
  --language LANGUAGE   Primary source language lane
  --source-name SOURCE_NAME
                        Optional source label for --content mode
  --include-extended    Enable GO and ZI overlays
  --deck-size DECK_SIZE
                        Maximum cards in the bounded deck
  --branch-width BRANCH_WIDTH
                        Near-domain branch width
  --execute             Execute the route through the same shell and include
                        route_run.json
  --timeout TIMEOUT     Execution timeout in seconds
  --json                Emit machine-readable JSON result

metadata.purpose: Package a source payload or brief for the Nexus Hub lane and optionally execute the route.
